standard_complex_multiplication.description: Standard complex multiplication requires 4 real multiplications and 2 additions
standard_complex_multiplication.hardware_cost: High - requires 4 multipliers per complex multiply

w_equals_i.hardware: Real-imaginary swap with negation of original imaginary part
w_equals_i.key_insight: Multiplication by i is a 90-degree rotation implemented as wire rearrangement

w_equals_minus_i.hardware: Real-imaginary swap with negation of original real part

critical_insight.explanation: The key mathematical insight is that multiplication by unit 4th roots {±1, ±i} eliminates all true multiplications. These are pure rotations and reflections that can be implemented with wire swapping and negation gates only.
critical_insight.hardware_implication: A complex-valued MAC operation with weights in {±1, ±i} requires ONLY additions, no multipliers whatsoever.

real_domain.operations: Requires addition and subtraction only for w ∈ {-1, +1}, but w=0 requires masking

complex_domain.operations: All weights require only wire swap and negation - NO multiplication
complex_domain.advantage: More expressive than ternary while still avoiding multiplication

explanation.summary: The Fairy ±i framework achieves addition-only inference by constraining all weights to unit 4th roots, which transform multiplications into simple wire operations.

detailed_mechanism.step_1: Weight encoding: Store each weight as 2 bits representing {±1, ±i}
detailed_mechanism.step_2: Activation representation: Keep activations in complex format (a + bi)
detailed_mechanism.step_3: Multiply-accumulate: For each weight-activation pair, perform wire swap/negation based on weight value, then accumulate
detailed_mechanism.step_4: Accumulation: Sum all transformed activations using standard addition

per_weight_activation_pair.w=-1: Negate both real and imaginary parts

hardware_operations.accumulation: Standard 2's complement addition of complex numbers

components.name: Negation Network
components.function: Apply negation based on weight and swap state
components.gates: XOR gates for conditional inversion + add-1 for 2's complement

components.name: Accumulator
components.function: Sum real and imaginary parts separately

data_path.description: 1. Decode weight[1:0] → control signals {swap, negate_real, negate_imag}
data_path.pipeline: 2. If swap: temp_real ← activation_imag, temp_imag ← activation_real
data_path.final: 3. If negate_real: temp_real ← -temp_real; If negate_imag: temp_imag ← -temp_imag

savings.for_8bit: Traditional: ~256 gates/mult × 4 + adders ≈ 1200 gates vs Complex-quantized: ~116 gates
savings.savings_ratio: ~10x reduction in gate count per MAC

power_savings.multiplier_elimination: ~80-90% of MAC power is in multipliers

cordic_potential.explanation: CORDIC is used for computing transcendental functions (sin, cos, atan) and arbitrary rotations using only shifts and adds. Since Fairy ±i uses only 90° rotations (multiples of i), CORDIC's iterative approach is overkill.

when_cordic_helps.scenario: If extending to higher-order roots of unity
when_cordic_helps.cordic_benefit: CORDIC could compute 45° rotations without multipliers

for_current_framework.direct_implementation: Wire swapping is simpler and faster than CORDIC for 90° rotations
for_current_framework.latency: Wire swap: 1 cycle vs CORDIC: 10-20 iterations
for_current_framework.recommendation: Use direct wire swap/negation, not CORDIC

option_1_hybrid_encoding.name: Complex-Ternary Hybrid
option_1_hybrid_encoding.advantage: Combines BitNet's sparsity advantage with complex rotation efficiency
option_1_hybrid_encoding.hardware: Add masking logic to complex MAC

option_2_extended_roots.name: 8th Roots of Unity
option_2_extended_roots.advantage: More expressive while still avoiding full multiplication

option_3_parallel_complex.name: Dual-Channel Complex BitNet
option_3_parallel_complex.architecture: Run two complex channels in parallel, combine outputs
option_3_parallel_complex.hardware_cost: 2x MAC units, but each still multiplication-free

feature.Hybrid: Combine both for best of both worlds

vs_standard_quantization.methodology: Compare Fairy ±i against standard INT4 and BitNet b1.58

perplexity.Fairy_pmi: Claimed comparable or better than standard quantization
perplexity.note: Complex representation may provide implicit regularization benefits

model_size.Fairy_pmi: 2 bits per complex weight (but represents complex number)

effective_information.geometric_interpretation: Complex representation captures phase information inherently

claims: Achieves full-precision accuracy in some benchmarks
verification_needed: Comparison with BitNet b1.58 on same model family

activation_precision.issue: Activations must be stored in complex format

initialization.issue: Complex-valued weights require special initialization schemes
initialization.implication: Training from scratch or complex-aware fine-tuning needed
initialization.mitigation: Convert existing real weights to complex domain

non_linearities.issue: Standard activations (ReLU, GELU) are designed for real numbers
non_linearities.mitigation: ModReLU, complex GELU variants exist in literature

recommendation.action: Obtain full Fairy ±i paper from Peking University
recommendation.rationale: Need complete mathematical details and benchmark results

recommendation.action: Evaluate Complex-Ternary Hybrid approach for Gate 0
recommendation.rationale: Combines best of BitNet sparsity with complex rotation efficiency

recommendation.action: Design and simulate complex MAC unit
recommendation.rationale: Verify gate count and power savings estimates
Explore collaboration or licensing for hardware implementation

recommendation.action: Evaluate extended roots of unity (8th roots)
recommendation.rationale: 3-bit weights with 8 states could provide quality boost while maintaining multiplication-free property
ADC-free MAC in memory could synergize with complex-valued operations

sources.title: Fairy ±i: First Complex-Valued LLM with 2-bit Quantization
sources.description: Original announcement of the Fairy ±i framework for complex-valued LLMs

sources.title: v13 Multilanguage Verification
sources.description: Verification of Chinese research claims including Fairy ±i

sources.title: BitNet b1.58 2B4T Technical Report
sources.description: Native 1-bit LLM with ternary weights for comparison

sources.title: Complex-Valued Neural Networks Survey
sources.note: General mathematical foundation for complex-valued deep learning

sources.title: CORDIC Algorithms for Complex Arithmetic
sources.note: Reference for rotation-based computation without multipliers
sources.applicability: Limited - 90° rotations are simpler with direct wire swap

representation.complex_number: z = a + bi where a, b ∈ ℝ, i² = -1

why_hardware_friendly.reason_1: Unit complex numbers are pure rotations - no scaling
why_hardware_friendly.reason_2: Multiplication by unit roots is isometric - no overflow growth
why_hardware_friendly.reason_3: 4th roots are simple 90° rotations - wire swap implementation
why_hardware_friendly.reason_4: No multiplication needed for weight-activation operation

phase_information.advantage: Complex representation captures phase relationships in data
phase_information.llm_relevance: Token embeddings may benefit from phase encoding

swap_mux.description: 2:1 MUX to swap real and imaginary based on weight bit
swap_mux.verilog_concept: assign out_real = weight[1] ? activation_imag : activation_real; assign out_imag = weight[1] ? activation_real : activation_imag;

conditional_negate.verilog_concept: assign neg_real = weight[0] ? ~swapped_real + 1 : swapped_real; assign neg_imag = weight[0] ^ weight[1] ? ~swapped_imag + 1 : swapped_imag;
